title: Front Facing Spreadsheet Upload

posts.author: mike.palmer (Mike Palmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, we are a product designer and have a board for new briefs being added.
I have created a form which is great for 1 off new product briefs.
However occasionally we have end users who want to have 10s or 100s of new products designed.

Q: is there anyway to have a front end spreadsheet upload to mass create items. Rather than a person in the team uploading it via the import new items spreadsheet?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Mike,

Would you be happy to elaborate a little further on what you mean by a front-end spreadsheet? Feel free to explain your ideal set up and I can get back to you on any potential ideas or workarounds!

posts.author: mike.palmer (Mike Palmer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca,

Sure no problem. Here is a map of my ideal process.
Submit a brief to create an item on a board.

  1. submit a single brief via a form (done)
  2. Submit a spreadsheet for multi line briefs

Spreadsheet Template with column headers matching Board.
Sales staff complete the spreadsheet template.
Sales staff head to a URL or an area in Monday to upload the spreadsheet

  • here is the kicker we don’t want them to head to the Board to upload for various reasons but the main one is to have a user friendly UX

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mike.palmer ,

I’m not sure that I got your case correct, but I feel like like the Smart Spreadsheet app can help with your case. You can connect a board from the 2nd step to a spreadsheet view (the board will be automatically recreated in the spreadsheet), then duplicate this sheet and make it a template. Or don’t connect a board but just create a spreadsheet view as a template with the columns matching a board.
You can play with the Smart Spreadsheet. app for free for 14 days and adapt it to your case. In case you will need more information, read the docs or reach out to our team.
